title: Quick Start
content:
# Quick Start

If you want to quickly experience building a blog with Kecare and don't plan to immediately study the generator's principles or engage in secondary development, following this documentation will allow you to run it locally.

---

## Prerequisites

### Required

| Requirement | Description | Verification Command |
|------|------|----------|
| Node.js | Used for installing dependencies and starting the development server | `node -v` |
| Theme Project | Responsible for rendering pages | - |

### Optional (Recommended)

| Requirement | Description |
|------|------|
| Git | Convenient for cloning the theme repository (you can also download and extract the ZIP file if not available) |

---

## Installing Kecare CLI

### Step 1: Execute the Installation Command

Open the terminal in any directory and execute:

```bash
npm create kecare@beta
```

### Step 2: Verify Installation

```bash
kecare -version
```

If the version information can be output, it indicates a successful installation.

---

## Get Example Themes

### Method 1: Using Git Clone

```bash
git clone https://github.com/Pamperkyumi/Kecare-theme.git
cd Kecare-theme
```

### Method 2: Download ZIP

1. Visit the [Kecare-theme repository](https://github.com/Pamperkyumi/Kecare-theme)
2. Click **Code** → **Download ZIP**
3. Extract the ZIP file and navigate into the directory

---

## Install Dependencies

### Step 1: Navigate to the Theme Directory

```bash
cd Kecare-theme
```

### Step 2: Install Project Dependencies

```bash
npm install
```

### Step 3: Install Kecare

```bash
npm install kecare@beta
```

> 💡 **Tip**: You can also use pnpm, yarn, or bun to install dependencies.

---

## Running the Generator

### Building a Site

Use the `kecare gen` command to generate the site:

```bash
# 在主题目录中
kecare gen .

# 或指定主题路径
kecare gen ./Kecare-theme

# 或使用绝对路径
kecare gen /path/to/Kecare-theme
```

### Start Development Server

```bash
kecare dev
```

---

## Verify Successful Startup

### View Output

If everything is normal, you will see output similar to:

```bash
$ nuxt dev
● Nuxt x.x.x ...
➜ Local: http://localhost:3000/
```

### Access Site

Open the browser and visit:

```
http://localhost:3000/
```

Being able to see the site homepage means you have successfully run it!

---

## Frequently Asked Questions

### Q: How to update Kecare?

A: Execute the following command:

```bash
npm update kecare@beta
```

Start your Kecare journey! 🚀
